intro: Torenia monroi, also known as Blue Skullcap, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide potential health benefits in treating conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Torenia monroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
